instruction: Replace the clear water only in the immediate vicinity of the beige hiking shoe with settled, opaque brown mud with muddy splash. Remove all frozen-in-time transparent splashes and droplets; instead show a stable muddy patch that has leveled out around the shoe. The shoe’s outsole and lower side are partially embedded and coated with a thin layer of wet mud; a few small specks cling to the laces and the rolled-up pant cuff. Rocks within this localized patch are smeared and partially covered by brown sludge, and the bed beneath the mud is no longer visible. Outside this roughly foot-width radius, keep the stream unchanged with clear water and visible stones. Use the same lighting and camera angle; replace water glare/refraction with a damp, low-gloss sheen on the mud. Add a natural transition edge where the mud meets the surrounding clear water, without any airborne droplets or splash arcs, depicting the final, stable state after the change.

prompts.superficial: Change the water surrounding the foot to mud.
prompts.intermediate: Convert only the liquid in direct contact with and immediately surrounding the hiking shoe into mud, respecting local physics: higher viscosity than water, opaque brown coloration due to suspended sediment, adhesion/wetting on the shoe and nearby rocks, gravity-driven settling and displacement under the shoe’s weight, and lighting interactions appropriate for muddy slurry. Keep the rest of the stream and environment unchanged.
prompts.explicit: Replace the clear water only in the immediate vicinity of the beige hiking shoe with settled, opaque brown mud with muddy splash. Remove all frozen-in-time transparent splashes and droplets; instead show a stable muddy patch that has leveled out around the shoe. The shoe’s outsole and lower side are partially embedded and coated with a thin layer of wet mud; a few small specks cling to the laces and the rolled-up pant cuff. Rocks within this localized patch are smeared and partially covered by brown sludge, and the bed beneath the mud is no longer visible. Outside this roughly foot-width radius, keep the stream unchanged with clear water and visible stones. Use the same lighting and camera angle; replace water glare/refraction with a damp, low-gloss sheen on the mud. Add a natural transition edge where the mud meets the surrounding clear water, without any airborne droplets or splash arcs, depicting the final, stable state after the change.

annotated_qa_pairs.question: Is there a contiguous patch of mud directly surrounding the shoe?
Do rocks in the region have mud on their surfaces?
